contract.title: Will the Federal Reserve Cut rates by 25bps at their January 2028 meeting?
contract.subtitle: Cut 25bps
contract.resolution_criteria: If the Federal Reserve does a Cut of 25bps on January 26, 2028, then the market resolves to Yes.

conditions.label: FOMC 25 bps cut on Jan 26, 2028
conditions.summary: On Jan 26, 2028, the FOMC announces an exact 25 bp reduction in the federal funds rate target range (both bounds down 0.25 pp vs prior), per official Federal Reserve release.

contract.narrative: Regulatory pressure is mostly indirect: capital/leverage rule changes and CRA shifts tweak credit and stability, with some easing for community banks but broader moves and balance‑sheet focus keeping policy tight. OFAC oil sanctions and tax cuts lean inflationary, lowering cut odds, while Waller’s pro‑cuts remarks and some stress actions nudge the other way. Decision remains pending to Jan 2028, driven by inflation and growth.

timeline.title: Treasury International Capital Data for March
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury — Office of Public Affairs
timeline.one_line_why: Monthly TIC capital‑flow data are macro background info that only indirectly and weakly affect Fed cut odds.

timeline.title: Isabel Schnabel: The quiet erosion of central bank independence
timeline.regulator: Bank for International Settlements
timeline.one_line_why: Argues central-bank independence is eroding, implying greater political pressure that modestly raises the chance the Fed could be pushed toward a rate cut by 2028.

timeline.title: Economic Fury Ramps Up Pressure on Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Oil Operations
timeline.regulator: Department of the Treasury — Office of Foreign Assets Control
timeline.one_line_why: OFAC sanctions on IRGC oil can tighten supply and lift oil-driven inflation, making a Jan 2028 Fed 25bp cut slightly less likely (indirect, modest effect).

timeline.title: Economic Fury Targets Iraqi Oil Official, Iran-Backed Terrorist Militias in Iraq
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control — Office of Foreign Assets Control
timeline.one_line_why: OFAC sanctions on Iraqi oil-linked actors could tighten oil supply and lift inflation, making a Jan‑2028 Fed cut slightly less likely.

timeline.title: Economic Policy Statements to TBAC: 2026 - 2nd Quarter​
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury
timeline.one_line_why: Quarterly Treasury economic policy statements give macro outlook but do not directly determine FOMC actions—only a weak, indirect signal for a 2028 cut.

timeline.title: Agencies Finalize Changes to Community Bank Leverage Ratio
timeline.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
timeline.one_line_why: Community bank leverage-rule changes affect lending/resilience and thus slightly influence Fed policy outlook, but are an indirect signal for a 2028 cut.

timeline.title: Community Bank Leverage Ratio: Final Rule
timeline.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
timeline.one_line_why: OCC's community bank leverage final rule could modestly constrain lending and slow growth, slightly raising probability of a Fed cut by Jan 2028.

timeline.title: Imposition of Special Measure Prohibiting Certain Transmittals of Funds Involving CIBanco S.A., Institución de Banca Multiple; Amendment
timeline.regulator: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
timeline.one_line_why: FinCEN action could modestly raise financial-market stress and slightly increase odds of Fed easing, but it's indirect and not decisive.

timeline.title: Over 53 Million Filers Claimed At Least One of President Trump’s Signature New Tax Cuts
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury
timeline.one_line_why: Widespread uptake of tax cuts could boost demand/inflation, slightly lowering the likelihood of a Fed 25bp cut by Jan 2028.

timeline.title: Stephen I Miran: Prospects for shrinking the Federal Reserve Bank's balance sheet
timeline.regulator: Bank for International Settlements
timeline.one_line_why: A 2026 BIS analysis on prospects for shrinking the Fed's balance sheet implies a continued tighter stance, modestly lowering odds of a Jan 2028 cut.
timeline.effective_date: some time after decision to proceed, likely well over a year and possibly several years

timeline.title: Agencies Request Comment on Proposals to Modernize the Regulatory Capital Framework And Maintain the Strength of the Banking System
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
timeline.one_line_why: Proposals to strengthen bank capital modestly reduce odds of a Jan‑2028 Fed cut by lowering financial‑stability risks and need for monetary easing.

timeline.title: Regulatory Capital: Category I and II Banking Organizations, Banking Organizations With Significant Trading Activity, and Optional Adoption for Other Banking Organizations
timeline.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
timeline.one_line_why: OCC capital-rule changes can modestly alter bank lending and financial conditions, indirectly nudging Fed policy odds but aren’t determinative for Jan 2028.

timeline.title: Regulatory Capital: Standardized Approach for Risk-Weighted Assets
timeline.regulator: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
timeline.one_line_why: OCC capital-rule changes can alter bank lending and macro conditions, indirectly nudging Fed policy long-term but not directly tied to the Jan 26, 2028 cut.

timeline.title: President Trump’s Tax Cuts are Putting More Money Back into the Pockets of American Families and Workers
timeline.regulator: U.S. Department of the Treasury
timeline.one_line_why: A Treasury press release about tax cuts is an indirect fiscal signal that could slightly raise growth/inflation expectations, modestly lowering cut odds.

timeline.title: Consolidated Reports of Condition and Income for Fourth Quarter 2025
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation — Division of Risk Management Supervision
timeline.one_line_why: FDIC Q4‑2025 call report is background banking‑health data; it’s only tangentially informative about Fed policy two years later and barely changes cut odds.

timeline.title: Agencies Issue Final Rule to Modify Certain Regulatory Capital Standards
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
timeline.one_line_why: Changes to bank capital rules indirectly affect financial conditions and the Fed's policy path but don't directly determine a Jan 26, 2028 25bp cut.

timeline.title: Agencies Issue Proposal to Enhance Community Banks’ Ability to Serve Their Communities While Maintaining Strong Capital Requirements
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
timeline.one_line_why: FDIC proposal easing community bank constraints may modestly reduce financial stress and the Fed’s need to cut rates by Jan 26, 2028 — weak indirect signal.
timeline.comment_deadline: 60 days after publication in the Federal Register

timeline.title: Christopher J Waller: The case for continuing rate cuts
timeline.regulator: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
timeline.one_line_why: Fed Governor Waller's 2025 speech favoring continued cuts raises odds of a cut by Jan 2028, but is an early, nonbinding signal.

timeline.title: FDIC Approves Notice of Proposed Rulemaking to Rescind the 2023 Community Reinvestment Act Final Rule
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
timeline.one_line_why: FDIC proposes rescinding the 2023 CRA rule, which could modestly ease bank lending and slightly reduce recession risk, marginally lowering odds of a Jan‑2028 Fed cut.
timeline.effective_date: April 1, 2026 or January 1, 2027 (most substantive provisions of 2023 CRA Final Rule would have become applicable)

timeline.title: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking on Adjusting and Indexing Certain Regulatory Thresholds
timeline.regulator: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation — Division of Risk Management Supervision
timeline.one_line_why: FDIC indexing of regulatory thresholds may modestly affect bank capital/stability and thus Fed policy, but it's indirect and unlikely to decisively change a Jan‑2028 cut.
timeline.effective_date: beginning of the first calendar quarter following adoption of the final rule

primary_drivers: Community bank leverage ratio final rule and related supervisory changes
Treasury sanctions/special measures restricting fund transmittals tied to Iran/Iraq oil
Macro/monetary signals: TIC capital flows, Fed balance-sheet shrinkage talk, central-bank independence concerns
heat_panel.explainer: Critical heat driven by overlapping regulatory and sanctions moves plus geopolitics — new community bank rules and Treasury special measures amid oil‑sanctions pressure and monetary scrutiny.
